prs.title: [ROCm] Fix AssertionError in ActivationQuantFusionPass when torch.compile is used on ROCm
prs.author: Bortlesboat
prs.body_head:
PR #38817 changed the `SiluMulBlockQuantPattern` registration guard from `is_cuda()` to `is_cuda_alike()`, inadvertently enabling it on ROCm. However, this pattern requires `per_token_group_fp8_quant` in `QUANT_OPS` (from `rms_quant_fusion.py`), and that op is CUDA-only (`#ifndef USE_ROCM` in `csrc/libtorch_stable/torch_bindings.cpp`).

On ROCm, `QUANT_OPS` does not contain `kFp8Dynamic128Sym` or `kFp8Dynamic64Sym`, so `ActivationQuantFusionPass.__init__` crashes when torch.compile is used:
```
AssertionError: unsupported quantization scheme kFp8Dynamic128Sym
```

The pattern can never match on ROCm anyway since the unfused `per_token_group_fp8_quant` op does not exist there. ROCm uses `rocm_aiter_fusion.py` for its own block-quant fusions.

Fix: revert the `SiluMulBlockQuantPattern` loop guard to `is_cuda()`. The `FUSED_OPS` entry for `silu_and_mul_per_block_quant` remains `is_cuda_alike()` for potential future use.

**Duplicate check:** No existing open PR addresses this. This is a regression introduced in #38817 (merged Apr 8, 2026).

**Tests:** `ActivationQuantFusionPass` is only instantiated during `torch.compile`; verified the guard logic by tracing `QUANT_OPS` population in `rms_quant_fusion.py` (lines 82-84, CUDA-only) against the `is_cuda_alike()` check.

**AI assistance:** Authored with Claude Code assistance.

cc @jeffdaily @sunway513 @jithunnair-amd @pruthvistony @ROCmSupport @gshtras @hongxiayang @naromero77amd

prs.title: [ROCm] Fallback SwigluOAI activation when _C op is absent
prs.author: Bortlesboat
prs.body_head:
## Summary
- add the same missing-`_C` fallback pattern to `SwigluOAIAndMul`
- switch `forward_cuda()` to use `self.op` so the class can cleanly fall back to `forward_native`
- add a focused regression test for ROCm/cuda-alike builds where `_C.swigluoai_and_mul` is absent

## Why this is not duplicating an existing PR
- I checked the live PR/issue surface for `swigluoai_and_mul` fallback work and did not find an open or merged PR covering this activation.
- This is a narrow follow-up to #39729, which applies the missing-`_C` fallback pattern to neighboring activations but does not touch `SwigluOAIAndMul`.

## Tests run
- `uv run --no-project --python 3.12 --with torch python -` with a stub loader around the real `custom_op.py` and `activation.py`
  - before the fix: reproduced `AttributeError: 'types.SimpleNamespace' object has no attribute 'swigluoai_and_mul'`
  - after the fix: `PASS`
- `uv run --no-project --python 3.12 python -`
  - `py_compile` succeeded for:
    - `vllm/model_executor/layers/activation.py`
    - `tests/kernels/core/test_activation.py`

## AI assistance
This PR was prepared with AI assistance.

prs.title: [ROCm] Add SWIGLUSTEP activation support to AITER fused MoE
prs.author: Bortlesboat
prs.body_head:
Step-3.5-Flash uses SWIGLUSTEP (SwiGLU with hard clamp at 7.0) for certain MoE layers. The AITER MoE backend doesn't handle this activation type, so running Step-3.5-Flash on ROCm with AITER enabled hits:

```
ValueError: Unsupported activation: MoEActivation.SWIGLUSTEP
```

This maps SWIGLUSTEP to the existing AITER swiglu activation path in both the dispatch and `_supports_activation`. Same approach used by cutlass_moe, fused_moe, and deep_gemm_moe backends which all support SWIGLUSTEP already.

Workaround before this fix: `export VLLM_ROCM_USE_AITER_MOE=0`

Fixes #36193

Signed-off-by: Bortlesboat <bortlesboat@users.noreply.github.com>

prs.title: [ROCm] Add missing @enable_fake_mode to TritonAddRMSNormPadFusionPass
prs.author: Bortlesboat
prs.body_head:
`RocmAiterTritonAddRMSNormPadFusionPass.__init__` is missing the `@enable_fake_mode` decorator that both sibling classes in the same file have:

- `RocmAiterRMSNormQuantFusionPass` (line 288) — has it
- `RocmAiterSiluMulFp8GroupQuantFusionPass` (line 389) — has it
- `RocmAiterTritonAddRMSNormPadFusionPass` (line 491) — **missing**

Without it, `get_inputs()` during pattern registration allocates real CUDA tensors via `self.empty(...)` and `torch.empty(...)` instead of fake tensors. This wastes GPU memory during compilation init and can fail if no GPU is available during tracing.

Also fixes a comment that incorrectly described the fused op's return order as \`(result, scale, residual)\` — the actual order from `_rocm_aiter_rmsnorm_with_add_fp8_group_quant_impl` is \`(result, residual, scale)\`.

No duplicate PRs found. AI assistance was used for code search; all changes reviewed by human submitter.

Signed-off-by: Bortlesboat <bortstheboat@gmail.com>

prs.title: [ROCm] Improve failed device detection diagnostics
prs.author: Bortlesboat
prs.body_head:
## Summary

Improve the `Failed to infer device type` error path so it reports the detected torch runtime and, when the environment looks like ROCm but torch does not expose HIP support, points users to the likely wheel/runtime mismatch instead of only telling them to enable debug logging.

This adds a small pure-Python helper for the diagnostic text and narrow regression coverage for the helper logic.

## Why this is not duplicate work

I checked:
- issue comments on #39378
- open PRs referencing `39378`
- open PRs searching for `Failed to infer device type`
- open PRs searching for `device type ROCm wheel`

The only open overlap I found is my docs PR #39639, which updates installation and troubleshooting docs for the same failure mode. This PR is the runtime-side complement: it changes the actual error users see when platform detection fails.

## Tests run

```bash
.venv/bin/python -m pytest --noconftest tests/config/test_device_diagnostics.py -q
.venv/bin/python -m py_compile vllm/config/device.py vllm/config/device_diagnostics.py tests/config/test_device_diagnostics.py
git diff --check
```

Results:
- `tests/config/test_device_diagnostics.py`: `3 passed`
- `py_compile`: passed
- `git diff --check`: passed, aside from CRLF warnings from this Windows checkout

## AI assistance

This PR was prepared with AI assistance. I reviewed the changes and test results before submission.

prs.title: [ROCm] Fix UnboundLocalError for prefix_scheduler_metadata in Triton attention
prs.author: Bortlesboat
prs.body_head:
Same fix as #39118 for rocm_attn.py: `prefix_scheduler_metadata` is only assigned in the `else` branch (when `use_cascade=False`) but referenced unconditionally in the `TritonAttentionMetadata` constructor. When cascade attention is active, this raises `UnboundLocalError`.

Initialize to `None` before the conditional, matching the pattern in `flash_attn.py` (line 464).

AI-assisted (Claude).

Signed-off-by: Andrew Barnes <andrew@bortlesboat.com>

prs.title: [ROCm] Fix UnboundLocalError for prefix_scheduler_metadata in cascade attention
prs.author: Bortlesboat
prs.body_head:
In `RocmAttentionMetadataBuilder.build()`, `prefix_scheduler_metadata` is only assigned inside the `else` branch (when `use_cascade=False`). When `use_cascade=True`, it's never set but passed to `RocmAttentionMetadata()` at line 156, which would raise `UnboundLocalError`.

Currently masked because `use_cascade_attention()` returns `False`, but this will crash the moment cascade is enabled for ROCM_ATTN (e.g., when prefix caching kicks in with shared prefixes).

Fix: initialize `prefix_scheduler_metadata = None` before the branch, consistent with how the other cascade variables are handled.

No duplicate PRs found. AI assistance was used for code search; all changes reviewed by human submitter.

Signed-off-by: Bortlesboat <bortstheboat@gmail.com>

prs.title: [ROCm] Use unified decode fallback for sliding-window AITER FA
prs.author: Bortlesboat
prs.body_head:
## Summary

The decode path in `rocm_aiter_fa.py` already documents that sliding-window attention should bypass `paged_attention_v1` and use the unified attention fallback, but the condition only checked small head sizes. This patch moves that rule into a small helper, applies it in the decode branch, and adds a narrow regression test that locks the sliding-window case in.

## Why this isn't duplicate work

- Checked nearby ROCm issue and PR activity for the same area, including `gh pr list --repo vllm-project/vllm --state open --search "sliding window unified_attention ROCM_AITER_FA"`
- Checked adjacent `rocm_aiter_fa` lanes such as `#33761`, `#39192`, and `#39527`
- The open work I found in this file is about KV-cache layout or broader kernel changes; I did not find an open PR that covers this decode fallback condition.

## Test Plan

- `wsl -e bash --noprofile --norc -lc 'cd /mnt/c/Users/andre/.config/superpowers/worktrees/vllm/vllm-rocm-aiter-fa-sliding-window-fallback && .venv/bin/python -m pytest --noconftest tests/v1/attention/test_rocm_aiter_fa_utils.py -q'`
- `wsl -e bash --noprofile --norc -lc 'cd /mnt/c/Users/andre/.config/superpowers/worktrees/vllm/vllm-rocm-aiter-fa-sliding-window-fallback && .venv/bin/python -m py_compile vllm/v1/attention/backends/rocm_aiter_fa.py vllm/v1/attention/backends/rocm_aiter_fa_utils.py tests/v1/attention/test_rocm_aiter_fa_utils.py'`
- `git diff --check`

## Test Result

- `tests/v1/attention/test_rocm_aiter_fa_utils.py`: `2 passed`
- `py_compile` passed for the touched code and test files
- `git diff --check` passed

## AI assistance

I used AI assistance to help draft and validate the patch, and I reviewed the diff and command output before opening this PR.

prs.title: [ROCm] Use quant_dtype in per_token_quant instead of hardcoded FP8
prs.author: Bortlesboat
prs.body_head:
`_rocm_aiter_per_token_quant_impl` and its fake both accept `quant_dtype` (which can be `torch.int8` or `FP8_DTYPE` per the assert), but hardcode `FP8_DTYPE` for the output tensor allocation. This means passing `torch.int8` would still get an FP8 tensor back.

Simple fix: use `quant_dtype` instead of `FP8_DTYPE` for `torch.empty()`.

Currently all callers pass `FP8_DTYPE` so this hasn't surfaced, but it's a correctness bug if int8 quantization is ever enabled on this path.

No duplicate PRs found (PR #36092 covers other _aiter_ops bugs but not this one). AI assistance was used for code search; all changes reviewed by human submitter.

Signed-off-by: Bortlesboat <bortstheboat@gmail.com>

prs.title: [ROCm][CI/Build] Guard gfx115x Docker base compatibility
prs.author: Bortlesboat
prs.body_head:
## Summary
- add a lightweight ROCm Docker config validator for `docker/Dockerfile.rocm_base`
- fail if `gfx1150` / `gfx1151` are advertised with a pre-`7.2` ROCm base image
- wire the check into pre-commit and cover the compatibility matrix with a focused unit test

## Why this is not duplicating an existing PR
Issue `#31333` reported that `docker/Dockerfile.rocm_base` was still on ROCm `7.0`. Current `main` is already on `rocm/dev-ubuntu-22.04:7.2.1-complete`, so this PR does **not** try to re-land that base-image bump.

Instead, this is a follow-up guard to keep that regression from coming back: if `PYTORCH_ROCM_ARCH` includes `gfx1150` / `gfx1151`, the new check rejects an older ROCm base image before it ships.

I also checked for an open PR tied to `31333` / this exact guard and did not find one.

## Testing
- `uv run --no-project --with pytest python -m pytest --noconftest tests/test_check_rocm_docker_config.py -q`
- `uv run --no-project tools/pre_commit/check_rocm_docker_config.py`
- `uv run --no-project --with pre-commit pre-commit run check-forbidden-imports --files tools/pre_commit/check_rocm_docker_config.py tests/test_check_rocm_docker_config.py`
- `uv run --no-project --with pre-commit pre-commit run check-rocm-docker-config --files docker/Dockerfile.rocm_base`
- `uv run --no-project --with ruff ruff check tools/pre_commit/check_rocm_docker_config.py tests/test_check_rocm_docker_config.py`
- `uv run --no-project --with ruff ruff format --check tools/pre_commit/check_rocm_docker_config.py tests/test_check_rocm_docker_config.py`

Note: I also tried a broader `pre-commit run --files ...` pass on the changed files, but on this Windows shell it hit an unrelated existing `/bin/bash` availability problem in the repo's `update-dockerfile-graph` hook. I am not claiming that broad hook pass is green here.

AI assistance was used for code search, issue/PR triage, and drafting the guard/test implementation; all changes were reviewed by the human submitter.

prs.title: [ROCm] Fix cu_seqlens_q off-by-one in AITER FA speculative decode path
prs.author: Bortlesboat
prs.body_head:
In the speculative decode path of `AiterFlashAttentionImpl` (when `decode_max_query_len > 1`), `cu_seqlens_q` is sliced as `query_start_loc[:num_decodes]`, giving `num_decodes` elements. But `cu_seqlens_q` for `unified_attention` is a cumulative length array that needs `num_seqs + 1` entries (including the leading 0).

The correct pattern is already used in the fallback path 33 lines later (line 1228-1229), which correctly does `[:num_decodes + 1]`.

Also fixed `descale_shape` which computed `shape[0] - 1` on the wrong-length slice, producing `num_decodes - 1` instead of `num_decodes`. Simplified to use `num_decodes` directly, matching line 1232.

No duplicate PRs found.

Testing:
- `uvx pre-commit run ruff-format --files vllm/v1/attention/backends/rocm_aiter_fa.py` (passes locally)
- Previous CI failure only requested the matching `ruff format` whitespace change on this line; this follow-up commit applies that exact diff.

AI assistance was used for code search and CI log review; all changes reviewed by the human submitter.

Signed-off-by: Bortlesboat <bortstheboat@gmail.com>

**Reference:** The correct `cu_seqlens_q` slicing is confirmed by the upstream AITER `unified_attention` implementation:
https://github.com/ROCm/aiter/blob/bc5ea32c19c604cda2f4781a97cb04d5fc494543/aiter/ops/triton/_triton_kernels/attention/unified_attention.py#L426

prs.title: Rocm/fused short seq attention
prs.author: MohamedSayedFathy
prs.body_head:
<!-- markdownlint-disable -->

## Purpose

Optimize PagedAttention v2 decode performance on ROCm for short sequences (≤256 tokens) by eliminating the reduce kernel when only a single partition is needed.

When `max_num_partitions == 1`, the QKV kernel writes directly to `final_out` instead of `tmp_out`, skipping:
- The `tmp_out` intermediate buffer write (~8 KB per seq/layer)
- The `tmp_out` read-back in the reduce kernel (~8 KB per seq/layer)
- The `exp_sums`/`max_logits` metadata writes (~256 bytes per seq/layer)
- The reduce kernel launch entirely (~10μs overhead)

This saves ~16.5 KB of HBM traffic per sequence per layer and eliminates one kernel launch.

### Key design choices:
- Uses `if constexpr (SINGLE_PARTITION)` for zero-cost abstraction — dead code eliminated at compile time
- Enabled by default, togglable via `VLLM_ROCM_FUSED_SHORT_SEQ=0` for A/B benchmarking
- Applied to both mfma4 (GQA ≤ 4) and mfma16 (GQA 5-16) kernels on MI300X
- No changes to the multi-partition path — long sequences are unaffected

## Test Plan

Verified on AMD MI300X by running `benchmark_paged_attention.py` with both `VLLM_ROCM_FUSED_SHORT_SEQ=1` (optimized) and `VLLM_ROCM_FUSED_SHORT_SEQ=0` (baseline). Both paths produce correct results with matching outputs.

```bash
# Optimized path (default)
python benchmarks/kernels/benchmark_paged_attention.py \
  --version v2 --custom-paged-attn --seq-len 128 --batch-size 8

# Baseline path (disabled)
VLLM_ROCM_FUSED_SHORT_SEQ=0 python benchmarks/kernels/benchmark_paged_attention.py \
  --version v2 --custom-paged-attn --seq-len 128 --batch-size 8

prs.title: [ROCm][Bugfix] Fall back when Quark MoE AITER dispatch is unsupported
prs.author: Bortlesboat
prs.body_head:
Fixes #40008.

This catches the Quark OCP MX AITER MoE runtime error `Unsupported kernel config for moe heuristic dispatch` and falls back to the existing emulation path instead of aborting the run.

Why this is not duplicating an existing PR:
- `gh issue view 40008 --repo vllm-project/vllm --comments`
- `gh pr list --repo vllm-project/vllm --state open --search "40008 in:body"` returned no matches.
- `gh pr list --repo vllm-project/vllm --state open --search "Unsupported kernel config for moe heuristic dispatch"` returned no matches.
- `gh pr list --repo vllm-project/vllm --state open --search "moe heuristic dispatch rocm"` only found #38665, which is a different ROCm lane.

Tests run:
- `wsl.exe bash -lc "cd /mnt/c/Users/andre/.config/superpowers/worktrees/vllm/vllm-rocm-moe-heuristic-40008 && PYTHONPATH=/mnt/c/Users/andre/.config/superpowers/worktrees/vllm/vllm-rocm-moe-heuristic-40008 uv run --no-project --python 3.12 --with torch==2.11.0 --with pytest --with-requirements requirements/common.txt --with-requirements requirements/test/cuda.in python -m pytest tests/model_executor/layers/test_quark_ocp_mx_moe.py -q"` -> `2 passed`
- `wsl.exe bash -lc "cd /mnt/c/Users/andre/.config/superpowers/worktrees/vllm/vllm-rocm-moe-heuristic-40008 && uv run --no-project --python 3.12 --with ruff ruff check vllm/model_executor/layers/quantization/quark/quark_moe.py tests/model_executor/layers/test_quark_ocp_mx_moe.py"` -> `All checks passed!`

AI assistance: I used Codex to help draft the patch and regression test, then reviewed the diff and local verification before opening this PR.

prs.title: [WIP] Add TRITON_MLA_SPARSE backend for SM80 sparse MLA support
prs.author: haosdent
prs.body_head:
## Purpose

Closes #38006. Enables sparse MLA models (GLM-5, DeepSeek-V3.2) on SM80 (A100/A800) and SM121 (GB10/DGX Spark), where DeepGEMM / FlashMLA-Sparse / FlashInfer-MLA-Sparse are unavailable.

Three changes:

1. **Guard fix.** `has_deep_gemm()` (package check) → `is_deep_gemm_supported()` (SM90+ arch check) in `sparse_attn_indexer.py` and `indexer.py`. Prevents DeepGEMM SM90+ kernels from being invoked on SM80/SM121 when the Python package happens to be installed.

2. **Triton indexer MQA logits.** `mqa_logits_triton.py` reproduces DeepGEMM's `fp8_mqa_logits` / `fp8_paged_mqa_logits` (bf16 tensor-core matmul with fp32 accumulator + scale, ReLU + per-head weighting, sum across heads). PyTorch reference stays in `mqa_logits_fallback.py`. On GB10, `fp8_paged_mqa_logits` at ctx=2048 goes from 6.4 ms (torch loop) → 0.03 ms (~250×), saving ~380 ms/token across 60 sparse layers.

3. **New `TRITON_MLA_SPARSE` backend** (`triton_mla_sparse.py` → `triton_sparse_mla_kernel.py`). Single pure-Triton kernel with split-KV for low-batch decode (`num_kv_splits > 1` when the single-pass grid under-fills the device) and a single-pass fast path otherwise. Stage 2 merges per-split `(out, lse)` tiles via online-softmax rescaling (pattern from `triton_decode_attention.py`). `@triton.autotune` over `(num_warps, num_stages)` × `(index_topk, NUM_KV_SPLITS)`; warmed in `TritonMLASparseImpl.__init__` so the first user request does not pay the sweep cost. Registered in `AttentionBackendEnum` and added to the non-SM100 MLA priority list.

**Kernel benchmark on NVIDIA A30 (SM80, 56 SMs):**

| shape | XPU kernel | this PR | speedup |
|---|---|---|---|
| nt=1, topk=2048, H=128 (decode) | 0.297 ms | 0.094 ms | **3.15×** |
| nt=1, topk=4096, H=128 | 0.580 ms | 0.167 ms | 3.46× |
| nt=1, topk=4096, H=16 | 0.582 ms | 0.081 ms | 7.17× |
| nt=128, topk=2048, H=128 (prefill) | 4.912 ms | 4.872 ms | 1.01× |

Decode (nt=1) across all (topk, H): median **4.30×**, best 7.17×. Non-decode (nt ≥ 8): autotu

prs.title: [CI/Build][AMD] Align LoRA %N shard ignore list with generic LoRA shard
prs.author: bedeks
prs.body_head:

Fixes [#40261](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/issues/40261) by aligning the AMD `LoRA %N` Buildkite step with the generic LoRA shard configuration.

prs.title: [KV Offload] Pass request context
prs.author: omerpaz95
prs.body_head:
## Purpose

Pass per-request context (`kv_transfer_params`) through the CPU offloading manager interface.

The `OffloadingManager` ABC and `CPUOffloadingManager` now accept a `ReqContext` argument on `lookup`, `prepare_load`, and `prepare_store`. `ReqContext` wraps `request.kv_transfer_params` (a free-form dict set via `sampling_params.extra_args`) and is constructed at each call site in `OffloadingConnectorScheduler`.

This is wiring-only: `ReqContext` is accepted but not yet consumed by `CPUOffloadingManager`.

Structural changes:
- `ReqContext` dataclass is defined in `vllm/v1/kv_offload/abstract.py` (shared layer) rather than `cpu/manager.py`, so other `OffloadingManager` implementations can use it without importing from the CPU-specific module.

## Test Plan

```bash
# Lint
cd vllm
pre-commit run

# Scheduler unit tests
pytest tests/v1/kv_connector/test_offloading_scheduler.py -v -s
```

## Test Result

Pre-commit (ruff, mypy, all hooks): all passed.

---
<details>
<summary> Essential Elements of an Effective PR Description Checklist </summary>

- [x] The purpose of the PR, such as "Fix some issue (link existing issues this PR will resolve)".
- [x] The test plan, such as providing test command.
- [ ] The test results, such as pasting the results comparison before and after, or e2e results
- [ ] (Optional) The necessary documentation update, such as updating `supported_models.md` and `examples` for a new model.
- [ ] (Optional) Release notes update. If your change is user facing, please update the release notes draft in the [Google Doc](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1YyVqrgX4gHTtrstbq8oWUImOyPCKSGnJ7xtTpmXzlRs/edit?tab=t.0).
</details>
